MDVP:Fo(Hz).definition: Average fundamental frequency of the voice — the perceived pitch.
MDVP:Fo(Hz).normal_range_adult: 85–255 Hz (adult typical range; varies by age and sex).
MDVP:Fo(Hz).interpretation: Markedly low or unusually variable Fo can correlate with vocal-fold instability.

MDVP:Fhi(Hz).definition: Maximum fundamental frequency observed in the recording.
MDVP:Fhi(Hz).normal_range_adult: Highly speaker-dependent; the dataset's 10th–90th percentile is 118–320 Hz.
MDVP:Fhi(Hz).interpretation: Used together with Fo and Flo to characterise pitch range.

MDVP:Flo(Hz).definition: Minimum fundamental frequency observed in the recording.
MDVP:Flo(Hz).normal_range_adult: Speaker-dependent; the dataset's 10th–90th percentile is 70–186 Hz.
MDVP:Flo(Hz).interpretation: Restricted pitch floor combined with low Fo can point to creaky/strained phonation.

MDVP:Jitter(%).definition: Cycle-to-cycle variation of fundamental frequency, expressed as a percentage of the average period.

MDVP:Jitter(Abs).definition: Absolute (microsecond) cycle-to-cycle variation of fundamental period.
MDVP:Jitter(Abs).interpretation: Same construct as MDVP:Jitter(%) but in absolute time units.

MDVP:RAP.definition: Relative Average Perturbation — three-period jitter smoothing.

MDVP:PPQ.definition: Five-point Period Perturbation Quotient — five-period jitter smoothing.
MDVP:PPQ.interpretation: Smoother estimator of pitch perturbation than raw jitter.

Jitter:DDP.definition: Average absolute difference of differences between consecutive periods.

MDVP:Shimmer.interpretation: High shimmer often co-occurs with breathiness or hoarseness.

MDVP:Shimmer(dB).definition: Cycle-to-cycle amplitude variation expressed in decibels.
MDVP:Shimmer(dB).interpretation: Same construct as MDVP:Shimmer but in dB units.

Shimmer:APQ3.definition: Three-point Amplitude Perturbation Quotient — three-period amplitude smoothing.
Shimmer:APQ3.interpretation: Reduces sensitivity to single-cycle amplitude artefacts.

Shimmer:APQ5.definition: Five-point Amplitude Perturbation Quotient — five-period amplitude smoothing.

Shimmer:DDA.definition: Average absolute difference of differences between amplitudes of consecutive periods.

NHR.definition: Noise-to-Harmonics Ratio — fraction of signal energy attributable to noise.
NHR.interpretation: Higher NHR indicates a noisier, less harmonic voice.

HNR.definition: Harmonics-to-Noise Ratio — clarity of voice in decibels.
HNR.interpretation: Lower HNR indicates more breathy or hoarse phonation.

RPDE.definition: Recurrence Period Density Entropy — measure of how regular cyclic patterns are.
RPDE.interpretation: Higher values suggest less periodic, more chaotic vocal cycles.

DFA.definition: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis — scaling exponent of stochastic self-similarity.
DFA.interpretation: Captures fractal-like structure of voice noise.

spread2.definition: Second nonlinear measure of fundamental-frequency variation.
spread2.interpretation: Higher values associated with more variability.

D2.definition: Correlation dimension — geometric complexity of the voice signal in phase space.
D2.interpretation: Higher D2 suggests more complex, less periodic dynamics.

PPE.definition: Pitch Period Entropy — entropy of the residual pitch signal after detrending.
PPE.interpretation: Often the single strongest feature for this dataset; high PPE indicates erratic pitch.
